phase-scope.phase-30: Design the runtime bridge verification gate before implementation.

V-M-044.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v

verification-levels.module-local: Runtime bridge tests use fake KPprotoN adapters and local DB sessions; they must not call live Telegram, DNS, host tools, deploy scripts, or install scripts.
verification-levels.wave-level: Runs with M-042/M-043 tests so bridge policy state cannot drift from assignment persistence or owner-safe payload generation.
verification-levels.phase-level: Phase-30 cannot pass unless bridge outage produces explicit degraded MTProto state while normal login, verified onboarding, and VPN config access remain non-blocked.

module-checks.check-1: apply-domain-policy succeeds for one issued SNI and records runtime policy state without persisting secrets.
module-checks.check-2: Startup replay loads active assignments idempotently and skips disabled or superseded assignments.
module-checks.check-3: Bridge unavailable, timeout, malformed response, or adapter exception returns degraded status without breaking KrotPN auth/VPN APIs.
module-checks.check-4: Scheduler reconciliation job is registered once and does not disturb existing subscription, cleanup, or anti-abuse jobs.
module-checks.check-5: Runtime logs and health payloads expose no tg://proxy links, derived secrets, base secret, salt, tokens, or provider credentials.
module-checks.check-6: Deploy/install surfaces remain unchanged; runtime bridge activation is configured inside backend/runtime service boundaries only.

scenarios.scenario-1: New active assignment is applied to MTProto policy runtime and returns activated status.
scenarios.scenario-2: Backend or bridge restart replays active SNI assignments exactly once per reconciliation cycle.
scenarios.scenario-3: Scheduled reconciliation repairs missing runtime policy state after local adapter reports drift.
scenarios.scenario-4: Runtime bridge failure marks MTProto proxy pending/degraded and emits safe operator evidence.
scenarios.scenario-5: Invalid SNI, disabled assignment, superseded assignment, or missing assignment is rejected before adapter calls.
scenarios.scenario-6: Policy apply failure does not prevent normal KrotPN login, verified-email activation, billing, or VPN config retrieval.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Policy logs may include assignment id, user id, SNI, status, adapter name, retry count, and correlation id.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Never log or return full tg://proxy links, derived secret, base secret, salt, JWT/session tokens, email verification tokens, or raw adapter credentials.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Health endpoints and admin summaries must report aggregate bridge state, not per-user proxy credentials.

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: policy_apply_success_count and policy_apply_failure_count, tagged by safe failure code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: bridge_health_status with last_success_at and last_failure_code without secret-bearing fields.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-30 may be executed autonomously only with fake/local KPprotoN adapters and isolated DB tests. Live runtime rollout, DNS/TLS edge mutation, host networking changes, and deploy/install script edits require explicit operator approval.
